scope_description: Independent review of newly unreviewed completed change sets: terminal-workspace-org, agent-card-legibility, embed-and-package-binary, release-v0-1-0, and binary-rename-herdr-go. Existing in-review Windows/rename release work is excluded.

excluded.reason: already covered by open review session review-v0-1-1-rc
already covered by open/approved release review sessions; not part of the new unreviewed batch

head: a6a02cac556b588815491464be72e9532f5c47a2

reviewer_manifest.execution_note: Six-reviewer full panel over frozen scope review-new-changesets-20260719; conditionals selected because the diff touches public API/runtime/install/release/external-process surfaces.

findings.title: Release packaging references deleted documentation files
findings.summary: The tag release workflow still copies docs/usage.md and docs/deployment.md, but this change set deletes both files. Release packaging will fail before any archive is created or uploaded.
findings.current_behavior: .github/workflows/release.yml copies docs/installation.md docs/usage.md docs/deployment.md into the release docs directory, while docs/usage.md and docs/deployment.md no longer exist in the tree.
findings.impact: Every tag-triggered release matrix job reaches the packaging step, cp exits non-zero for the missing docs, and no downloadable release asset is published.
findings.scenario: A maintainer pushes a v* tag after these changes. Build succeeds, then the Package step fails at the docs copy line, leaving the installer without a matching release archive.

evidence.note: docs/usage.md and docs/deployment.md are deleted in this cumulative diff.

findings.proposed_fix: Update the release packaging manifest to copy only documentation paths that exist, or restore the deleted docs if they remain part of the release artifact contract.
acceptance_criteria: Release package step no longer references deleted files.
A focused shell check proves every path copied from docs exists before upload.

findings.title: Installer migrates legacy state before proving a replacement binary is available
findings.summary: install.sh moves legacy config/data before the release archive has been downloaded, extracted, and validated.
findings.current_behavior: The migration calls run before mktemp, curl, tar, and archive content checks.
findings.impact: A failed upgrade can leave legacy state moved even though no new herdr-go binary was installed.
findings.scenario: An existing herdr-gateway install has legacy config/data. The user runs install.sh during a transient GitHub outage; legacy directories are renamed first, then curl fails and exits.
findings.proposed_fix: Download and validate the release archive first, then migrate state immediately before the final install/service switch, with rollback or preserve-on-failure behavior for partial migrations.
acceptance_criteria: Download/archive failures leave legacy config and data untouched.
Migration happens only after replacement artifacts are known-good.

findings.title: Installer moved to download-only while docs and prior plan still require source fallback
findings.summary: install.sh now exits when the release download, extraction, or archive-shape check fails. The approved embed/package plan and parts of the current installation spec still describe a cargo+npm source-build fallback.
findings.current_behavior: install.sh resolves a release URL, runs curl, extracts the archive, checks for herdr-go, and dies on any failure. No cargo/npm fallback branch remains.
findings.impact: A host with a valid Rust/Node toolchain cannot install when GitHub is unavailable, the latest release lacks a matching asset, or the archive shape is wrong, despite the documented fallback contract.
findings.scenario: The latest GitHub release is missing herdr-go-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz. install.sh exits at the curl line and points to source-build docs instead of building from the local checkout or an explicit fallback path.
findings.proposed_fix: Either restore a correctly ordered source-build fallback after failed prebuilt resolution, or update the locked/product docs to make download-only no-clone installation the explicit contract and verify that behavior.
acceptance_criteria: The installer behavior and specs agree on whether fallback exists.
A focused test covers the no-asset/download-failure case.

findings.title: Dev deploy migrates live state before dependency install and compile can fail
findings.summary: dev-deploy.sh moves legacy config/data before npm install, web bundling, and cargo release compilation.
findings.current_behavior: The migration calls run immediately after systemd preflight, before dependency/build commands.
findings.impact: A failed local deploy can move state out from under the currently working legacy service without ever producing a new runnable service.
findings.scenario: npm install fails because the registry is unavailable. The script exits before service switching, but legacy config/data have already been moved.
findings.proposed_fix: Complete dependency install, bundling, and compilation first; move state immediately before service activation, with rollback for partial migration or activation failure.
acceptance_criteria: Build failures leave legacy state untouched.
State migration occurs only after the new binary and UI bundle exist.

findings.title: Embedded-asset tests depend on local static bundle residue
findings.summary: The Rust test path asserts embedded UI content is non-empty, but build.rs only guarantees that static/ exists. A clean checkout without a bundled UI is not isolated in CI.
findings.current_behavior: build.rs creates an empty static directory; src/web/mod.rs has tests that expect embedded index content to exist; the Rust CI job does not bundle the web UI before cargo test.
findings.impact: Local tests can pass because an untracked static bundle exists, while a clean checkout can fail tests or compile a binary with no usable embedded UI.
findings.scenario: A new CI runner checks out the repo without static/index.html, runs the Rust job, and the embedded UI test fails or the binary embeds an empty asset set.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a clean exported-tree CI or script proof with static absent, then provide a deterministic fixture or explicitly make no-bundle Rust tests valid.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh-checkout/no-static behavior is tested in isolation.
Rust tests do not depend on untracked local bundle residue.

findings.title: Switcher behavior changes lack DOM-level regression coverage
findings.summary: The switcher changed title fallback, caption composition, grouping, collapse state, and selection behavior, but tests cover only pure helper output.
findings.current_behavior: web/test/switcher.test.ts checks helper-level grouping/color behavior, not rendered DOM or click/refresh behavior.
findings.impact: Cards can render blank titles, dangling separators, wrong collapsed defaults, or wrong selected rows while tests stay green.
findings.scenario: Two workspaces are sorted alphabetically for display; clicking a rendered card selects the wrong original row due to index drift, and no DOM test catches it.
findings.proposed_fix: Add jsdom tests around the rendered switcher with mocked API responses covering zero/one/two workspaces, title/tab fallbacks, collapse behavior, refresh, and selection identity.
acceptance_criteria: One-workspace and multi-workspace DOM branches are covered.
Selection identity is asserted after grouped sorting.

findings.title: Upgrade stops existing services without activating the replacement
findings.summary: install.sh disables/stops conflicting services and only enables herdr-go.service; it does not start or restart the replacement.
findings.current_behavior: The script runs systemctl disable --now for legacy/dev units, writes/enables herdr-go.service, and prints a manual start command.
findings.impact: An upgrade from a running legacy service can take the gateway offline until the operator notices and manually starts the new unit.
findings.scenario: A user upgrades from herdr-gateway.service while relying on the phone UI. install.sh stops the old service, enables the new unit, exits successfully, but leaves no running gateway.
findings.proposed_fix: Prepare files first, then perform the service switch as the last step with enable --now or restart. If activation fails, report clearly and consider restarting the previously active unit.
acceptance_criteria: A successful upgrade leaves the replacement service running.
Service stops happen after replacement artifacts and unit content are ready.

findings.title: Installer prints the generated login token to terminal output
findings.summary: install.sh prints the generated HERDR_GO_WEB_SECRET value on first install. This is documented behavior, but it increases exposure through terminal scrollback and captured automation logs.
findings.current_behavior: When a new herdr-go.env is created, install.sh prints Login token: <token>.
findings.impact: A terminal recorder, support transcript, or shared install log can reveal the web login token.
findings.scenario: An operator runs the installer inside a logged terminal session and later shares the log while the token remains valid.
findings.proposed_fix: Consider printing only the protected file path plus a local retrieval command, or explicitly accept first-install token display as a product tradeoff.
acceptance_criteria: Token display behavior is either removed or explicitly accepted in security-facing docs/specs.

findings.title: Accent-color tests are weak and partly tautological
findings.summary: The switcher accent-color tests compare a function result with itself and accept any numeric HSL triple.
findings.current_behavior: A constant color or invalid hue range could satisfy the current assertions.
findings.impact: The kind-derived visual distinction can regress without a test failure.
findings.scenario: kindAccent is changed to return the same color for all kinds; tests still pass because they do not assert representative expected outputs or distinctness.
findings.proposed_fix: Assert fixed representative outputs, hue bounds, and distinct outputs for selected different kinds.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail for a constant accent implementation.

findings.title: Release packaging references deleted documentation files
findings.resolution: Release packaging now copies only docs/installation.md from top-level docs, and rename_contract.sh guards every docs/*.md path in the release Package step against missing files.

evidence.related: tests/rename_contract.sh:6-12; bash tests/rename_contract.sh and full project verify passed on 2026-07-19

findings.title: Release packaging references deleted documentation files
findings.resolution: Release packaging now copies only docs/installation.md from top-level docs. rename_contract.sh guards every docs/*.md literal in the release Package step against missing files and every docs markdown glob against matching no files.

evidence.related: tests/rename_contract.sh:6-17; bash tests/rename_contract.sh and full project verify passed on 2026-07-19

gate4.reason: The only P1 finding was fixed and delta-reviewed; P2/P3 findings remain recorded as non-blocking backlog.

decision.summary: Review-new-changesets-20260719 is approved after resolving the release packaging P1. Current verify is green; remaining 6 P2 and 2 P3 findings are non-blocking backlog.
